fundamentals.name: Snowflake Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Snowflake Inc. provides a cloud-based data platform for various organizations in the United States and internationally. The company's platform includes artificial intelligence (AI) Data Cloud, which enables customers to consolidate data into a single source of truth to drive meaningful business insights, build data applications, and share data and data products, as well as applies AI for solving business problems. It serves financial services, advertising, media and entertainment, retail and consumer goods, healthcare and life sciences, manufacturing, technology, telecom, travel and hospitality, and government and defense industries, as well as the public sector. the company has a collaboration with OpenAI, L.L.C. for the development of AI solutions for joint enterprise customers that deliver tangible return on investment. The company was formerly known as Snowflake Computing, Inc. and changed its name to Snowflake Inc. in April 2019. Snowflake Inc. was incorporated in 2012 and is based in Menlo Park, California.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and free cash flow, but currently unprofitable and trading at a very high valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 21.9%; next earnings date is 2026-08-26.
positives: Revenue growth 33.5% YoY indicates strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow of $1.74B TTM demonstrates healthy cash generation.
Analyst consensus is 'strong_buy' with a mean target suggesting potential upside.
Last quarter's EPS beat by 21.9% shows positive earnings momentum.
negatives: Operating margin of -22.2% and profit margin of -23.8% indicate unprofitability.
Return on equity of -54.9% and return on assets of -9.7% are significantly negative.
Forward P/E of 107.40 and Price/Sales of 19.32 represent a very high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 142.91 suggests significant leverage.
Current ratio of 1.05 and quick ratio of 0.94 indicate tight liquidity.

news.paragraph: Snowflake's stock is experiencing a significant rally, fueled by positive commentary from financial analysts like Jim Cramer and Dan Ives, who see upside driven by the company's AI strategy and its substantial investment in AWS. The narrative around Snowflake's competitive moat is being redefined by its AI initiatives, contributing to strong performance and analyst upgrades. This positive sentiment follows a recent earnings beat, with the company exceeding expectations for its last reported quarter.
